title: QUIC RFC 9000 STREAMS_BLOCKED Runtime Emission Work Item
summary: Emit bounded protected `STREAMS_BLOCKED` advisory packets when active outbound stream opens are blocked by the peer's current stream limit while keeping the open request pending until real `MAX_STREAMS` growth.
planned_changes:
- Refine the API pending-open requirement so advisory `STREAMS_BLOCKED` signaling no longer conflicts with the pending-open completion contract.
- Add a runtime `STREAMS_BLOCKED` payload builder and guarded send path beside the existing blocked-signal send helpers.
- Attempt advisory emission when a queued outbound open is blocked by the peer stream limit, while preserving the pending request.
- Suppress duplicate advisory emission while ack-eliciting packets or retransmissions are already outstanding.
- Add positive and negative requirement-home tests that open the protected packet and verify the STREAMS_BLOCKED direction and Maximum Streams value.
- Add permanent BenchmarkDotNet coverage for STREAMS_BLOCKED frame parse and format hot paths.
verification_plan: Run the focused RFC 9000 S4P6/S13P3/S19P14 requirement-home tests, run the adjacent API pending-open regression tests, run the STREAMS_BLOCKED frame-codec BenchmarkDotNet suite with Dry validation, render/check the touched SpecTrace artifacts, and keep the scope limited to advisory runtime emission.
out_of_scope: Completing a blocked open without real `MAX_STREAMS` growth.
completion_notes: Implemented bounded active-runtime `STREAMS_BLOCKED` emission with protected-packet proof coverage, retained the pending-open completion boundary, and added BenchmarkDotNet Dry coverage for the STREAMS_BLOCKED frame-codec hot path.
